holdings.infoBox: MOAT is an equity ETF with a total of 58 individual holdings. The top holdings are Fortinet stock at 4.40%, NXP Semiconductors at 3.48%, Masco at 2.95%, NVIDIA at 2.79%, and Mondelez International at 2.77%.

overview.description: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (MOAT) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Morningstar Wide Moat Focus index. The fund tracks a staggered, equal-weighted index of 40 US companies that Morningstar determines to have the highest fair value among firms with a sustainable competitive advantage. MOAT was launched on Apr 25, 2012 and is issued by VanEck.

data.title: VanEck Enters Canada, Appoints Industry Vet Kevin Gopaul as CEO
data.text: VanEck has appointed Kevin Gopaul as CEO of VanEck Canada. According to the press release, Gopaul brings more than 25 years of industry experience to the newly created role.

data.title: VanEck Announces Year-End Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs
data.text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--VanEck announced today the 2025 annual distributions per share for its VanEck equity exchange-traded funds.

data.title: Top 3 best ETFs for value investors in 2025
data.text: Exchange-traded funds (ETFs) had a strong performance in 2024 as inflows surged by over $1 trillion. Most of these inflows were in active and crypto-focused funds like Bitcoin's IBIT and FBTC.

data.text: Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) are some of the best assets to invest in, especially for long-term investors. These funds offer diversification, often at a low cost.

data.text: 2023 was a strong year for American and some European equities. In the US, the closely-watched S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Dow Jones ended the year at their all-time highs.
